toc.value: 1) Confirm you have PWM fan control in Armbian
2) Dump the running device tree and locate the fan node
4) Create a Device Tree Overlay to set your fan curve
A) Choose your fan node path
8) Watch PWM change in real time

frontMatter.title: How to Tune CPU FAN Speed With PWM on Armbian (cooling-levels based on Temperature)
frontMatter.author: Amrut Prabhu
frontMatter.summary: Tune Armbian’s PWM fan curve using a Device Tree Overlay. This step-by-step guide shows how to locate the pwm-fan node, edit cooling-levels and temp-trips, compile a .dtbo overlay, enable it in armbianEnv.txt, and verify fan speed changes in Linux

Radxa Cubie A5E Hands-On: The Budget NVMe SBC
How to Back Up an SD Card (Full OS Image) to an IMG File Using Linux

prev.title: How to Auto-Mount an NVMe Drive on Linux at Startup (Using /etc/fstab)
prev.author: Amrut Prabhu
prev.summary: Auto-mount your NVMe SSD on Linux at startup using /etc/fstab the right way. Learn how to find the UUID, create a mount point, add a safe fstab entry

next.author: Amrut Prabhu

CPU.Cores:
2 × Cortex-A76 up to 2.0GHz
6 × Cortex-A55 up to 1.8GHz

specifications.Storage:
MicroSD
Onboard UFS module (optional, up to 1TB)
NVMe SSD (via PCIe FPC + external HAT)
specifications.Video Output:
1 × Micro HDMI 2.0 up to 4K@60fps
1 × USB-C (DP Alt Mode) for external displays
specifications.USB:
1 × USB-C 2.0 OTG (Power + Data)
1 × USB-C (USB 3.1) with DisplayPort Alt Mode
specifications.Power:
5V via USB-C power port
5V via GPIO pins 2 & 4
